title: Programs
meta_description: Sounds of Saving works with artists, schools, and live events to help young people use music to take care of their mental health. Explore our programs across film, education, and community.
heading: Programs
lead: We help young people use music to take care of their mental health. That work runs on a simple loop: artists tell the truth about a hard time and the song that got them through it, we turn those stories into a music-based, SEL-rooted curriculum for schools, and we show up at live events with real resources and a way to get help. Every program below is one part of that loop.

steps.label: Listen
steps.body: An artist shares the song that helped them through a hard time, on film and in person.

steps.label: Learn
steps.body: Those stories become a classroom curriculum that teaches students to use music for their wellbeing.

steps.label: Connect
steps.body: We bring it into the room at live events, with a mental-health professional and a path to care.

groups.label: Listen
groups.heading: Artist storytelling and media
groups.intro: Intimate films and content where artists are honest about mental health, made to connect with the people who already love their music.

cards.title: Song That Found Me at the Right Time
cards.what: A film series where an artist covers a song that helped them through a difficult time and talks about what else helped: therapy, meditation, movement, community.
cards.who: Young people who find it easier to hear a hard truth from an artist they trust than from a pamphlet.
cards.happens: We record an intimate cover and conversation with each artist, then publish it across YouTube and social. The series is produced in partnership with the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline and their sister organization Vibrant Emotional Support.
cards.proof: Featured in Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, AfroPunk, and MTV. Artists include Sharon Van Etten, Meshell Ndegeocello, Bartees Strange, SASAMI, and CHAI.

cards.title: Social Impact Media
cards.what: Personal, intimate content built with partners to connect with fan bases through relatable stories and a shared connection to music.
cards.who: Artists, labels, and mission-aligned organizations who want to make mental-health content that does not feel like a PSA.
cards.happens: Formats span long-form films (5 to 7 minutes), cross-platform social cuts for YouTube, Meta, TikTok, and Snap, short PSAs (30 to 90 seconds), and live activations, often tied to cultural moments like Mental Health Awareness Month, Suicide Prevention Month, and World Mental Health Day.
cards.proof: Built on the same storytelling craft as the 988 series.

groups.label: Learn
groups.heading: Education and engagement
groups.intro: A music-based, SEL-rooted curriculum that teaches students to use music intentionally for their mental health, in high schools and on college campuses.

cards.title: SoS HS
cards.what: A limited high-school curriculum, rooted in Social and Emotional Learning, that teaches students how to use music receptively and intentionally for their mental health and wellbeing.
cards.who: High-school counselors, SEL leads, and educators looking for an engaging, culturally relevant wellness curriculum.
cards.happens: 4 to 5 sessions across the school year, plus supplemental material for student-led touchpoints between sessions.
cards.proof: Now accepting pilot partners for the 2026 to 2027 school year.

cards.title: SoS Uni
cards.what: A two-pronged campus initiative that pairs awareness with community building.
cards.who: College wellness staff, campus radio, and student clubs.
cards.happens: An annual College Campus Radio Campaign anchored by peer and artist storytelling and music-based prompts, plus live events and music-based community-building experiences for campus clubs and groups.
cards.proof: Now accepting pilot partners for the 2026 to 2027 school year.

cards.title: Bring an Artist Workshop
cards.what: One-off workshops that use live performance, discussion, and storytelling to connect students to local mental-health professionals and pathways to care.
cards.who: Schools and campuses that want a single high-impact event rather than a full curriculum.
cards.happens: An artist-led session built around a mental-health theme, with a clear handoff to local care.
cards.proof: Past artists include Erick the Architect, Pink Siifu, Cleo Reed, Bryndon Cook, deem spencer, and Maassai.

groups.label: Connect
groups.heading: Events and live work
groups.intro: We show up where young people already are, at shows, on tours, and at live conversations, with real resources and a way to get help.

cards.title: Events and Feedback Sessions
cards.what: A suite of events, from showing up on-site with mental-health resources to leading our live Feedback Sessions, where music meets a guided conversation about a mental-health theme.
cards.who: Music fans at the events, plus artists, writers, and mental-health professionals who take part.
cards.happens: Feedback Sessions bring together musicians, artists or writers, and mental-health professionals around a theme. Past themes have included getting un-stuck from inertia, gender identity, working through childhood trauma, and coping with pandemic-related depression and anxiety. They stay intimate and conversational, moving from naming a problem to ways of coping with it.
cards.proof: Produced with ongoing partners including OFFAIR, Republic Records, and National Sawdust.

cards.title: SoS On Tour
cards.what: We join tours to bring education and resources to places where mental healthcare is scarce and hard to navigate.
cards.who: Fans at tour stops, especially in areas where local care is hard to find.
cards.happens: At each stop we partner with a local mental-health professional and a member of the 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline, who help fans navigate local resources and demystify getting help. We hand out wallet crisis cards with a QR code to local resources, and collect Songs That Found Me cards with fans' own stories.
cards.proof: Tours joined include Courtney Barnett's Here and There Festival, Re:SET, and 6LACK's Since I Have a Lover.

educators_pointer.title: Bringing SoS into a school or campus?
educators_pointer.body: We have a dedicated space for school counselors, SEL leads, and college wellness staff: the two tracks, what you get, the proof, and an intake form.
